reviews.review_description: Been over 3 years since any new content or updates have came to this game. I originally loved this game, however, the lack of new updates or new content coming….there’s absolutely no reason for that. Like wtf are you developers doing?? BUILD YOUR GAME!!!! This is inexcusable and unacceptable!!

reviews.review_title: Great game with a steep curve
reviews.review_description:
At first I wasn't sure about the way the game handled but after a couple of songs you start to get a bit of a feel for it. Overall it's a beautifully made game with some serious Rock Band vibes and there's a pretty decent song selection.

However there is a steep learning curve and combined with some issues with the way guitar and hand tracking works, it can be really overwhelming trying to level up your gameplay.

First, the guitar itself freely moves up and down as you move your fret hand towards and away from you. This sometimes results in a mismatch of your pick hand and the hitbox for the strings. There could be a dead zone or the option of having the guitar stay fixed to help with that issue.

Secondly the leap in technique from easy to normal is big. Going from playing two finger patterns to multiple, constantly shifting patterns you have never tried before at a much higher rate of speed. There needs to be an in between, learning the different finger patterns at a comfortable speed etc. The rate those notes come at you is hard to take in when the changes are so frequent. Some people will take to this but the average user will struggle to keep up.

Finally, there is definitely an issue with the game sometimes missing correct placement of fingers. It completely killed a good run for me because it refused to recognise a simple three finger pattern on repeat.

I still mostly enjoy what I've played so far but I think it'll be limited due to these issues. You can get some good fun and nostalgia from playing this and when it works it's really satisfying, but if you're considering it be prepared for a tough challenge and some frustration too.

Overall, a generally well made game with some issues that could be addressed if the devs care to do so.

Wow... That's all I can say is wow... This is Guitar Hero on steroids, brought back so many memories, I'm glad someone out there made this game, endless fun and amazing hand tracking and virtual world and experience, try it today
So fun especially if your a new guitarist it’s not rockstar realistic 😂😂But definitely will give you a good boost to learn

The tutorial level only teaches me to place my fingers in the correct positions and then strum the strings. But when I finished the tutorial and played two beginner songs, I met some colored lines on the chart and I had no idea how to handle them. If I enter the practice room, there are some abstract paintings on the wall that seem to explain how to handle the lines, but they are too abstract. As a player with no guitar experience, I simply can't understand. In addition, during my third round, the guitarist came out and talked about 'energy' and 'pose.' I couldn't keep up at the time, so I hoped to listen to it again. However, I searched the entire game and couldn't find a way to replay that message. Such important information about the basic gameplay mechanics should not be unavailable for players to replay. If the developers can improve the incomplete tutorial, this game has potential. However, in its current state, it only leaves us feeling confused.

I love this game so much don't get me wrong but we need for van halen, Ozzy , zz top , also through the fire and the flames, Linkin park, eagles, we need this to be like beat saber but fire rock and roll songs bc this game is so amazing

reviews.review_title: Pretty good but a few complaints
reviews.review_description:
I’m writing this from a real world guitar point of view. The game is pretty good at capturing the feel of playing however, I have a few nit-picks. Firstly the song selection, there are very few songs to choose from. Although the songs are popular and recognisable ones, there are not many of them. Would be nice to see more in the future. 

Secondly, this is very nit-pickey but the size of the guitar is a bit smaller than a real one. I’m aware this is probably to suit the younger players too but it also makes it that extra bit harder for the real world players. 

Lastly, the hand tracking. I use the Quest 2 and the tracking works 99% of the time however I still miss notes from the picking hand which should not happen. That 1% can make the difference.

Overall the game is really good and captures the feel nicely but not perfect. Would still recommend this game though.
Just wish they had more songs and if the creator sees this get American rock band Wheatus teenage dirtbag
There good song but add Metallica or acdc

The beatmap designs are really good. I play on hard.

BUY THIS GAME. ITS 1000% WORTH. HAND TRACKING OR CONTROLLER. 

I seriously hope that one day, they get so big and so well known, they start making physical guitar controllers for pc and hell, even quest 2. 

Now i have a few things i would love to address and hopefully we see a solution in the future

One, when i play a song and I take my headset off and put it on, sometimes my hand glues to the guitar head and it wont fix unless i go back to main menu. 

Second, using a controller, we need some adjustment slider for your wrist turn cause sometimes i be breaking my wrist trying to flick so deeply. I would like to tune the twist at least 

Third. The ability to slightly tilt the guitar forward or inward. I like adjusting the position of the guitar, but i also want to be able to tilt it a little just so strumming is a bit easy. Idk if this makes sense but yeah.


10/10 would buy again, but i dont have to cause its cross buy. W developer.

Update 2025 July 29th

What happened??? Where did the support go? Y'all had this in the bag, please don't tell me y'all folded 😕

This game has potential. The problem is you can’t move the guitar. Whatever position it’s in when it pops up is the position you have to play it in. Sometimes it’s sideways, sometimes it’s way too low and sometimes it’s way too high. I literally had to stand on my couch sideways once to play it.  And there’s times when you’re playing and you lose your position because the guitar moves itself and there’s times it says you missed when you are on the right colors.

developer_response.body:
Hey there!

You can move the guitar by grabbing the tremolo bar! Up, down, or in any direction you would like. You can also recenter the view by pinching your index and thumb fingers of your non-dominant hand towards you.

We hope this was helpful; we hope to see you soon!

reviews.review_description: So fresh gameplay for those who love guitar hero and rock band.

I had huge, high hopes for this. As a huge guitar hero player back in the day and later on Rock Band, and as a guitarist in real life, I was hoping this would be closer to Rocksmith than rock band. 

Hand tracking: Really, really good. But, my issue isn’t the fingering or frets, it’s the guitar placement and strumming. The strumming didn’t really match the patterns of the song and they felt really, really awkward. 

Difficulty: Guys, can we get a few songs in before introducing and requiring hammer-ons (as of now, hadn’t encountered pull-offs yet). The finger patterns and stuff are good, but man…if you get groovin’ and turn, well, the notes slide gets all twisted and stuff. 

It works for now, but my frustrations really prohibit me from playing it too much. I really, really want it to be good, but the guitar placement, wonky strum patterns, and disappearing fingerboard tracks is a real bummer. I’ll keep
My eyes peeled for updates.

developer_response.body:
Update: LATENCY FIXED!!

Last Friday, we updated the game based on your concerns and thoughts. Thanks! These are the things we have improved: 

	- Better the audio feedback on played and missed notes. The guitar will stop playing when you mess it. 
	- Better audio sync; Music latency is so much better now! The notes match with the music by default!!
	- Better strumming accuracy
	- Smoother guitar movement when playing to make the experience way more stable. 
	- Cloud saves. Never lose your progress. 

We also fixed several bugs reported by the community.
We hope to see you back on the stage soon to feel the improvements to Unplugged. 
Thanks for your patience.

reviews.review_title: This game makes me feel perfect.
reviews.review_description: This game is so awesome. I never knew I was this good. Anyway, there is a bug that when I pause the game, the pause menu gets too far away from me to reach it. Can you fix that for me please? Thank you.

developer_response.body:
Hi ShrunkenAura!

Thanks a lot for the feedback here! We noticed this bug, and it will be solved for the next update. Sorry for the inconvenience.

We're always looking to improve Unplugged and are constantly tuning the game based on the community's feedback, so if you have any other concerns or questions, please let us know at support@mustbeanotherway.com.

reviews.review_title: You know you are going to buy it.
reviews.review_description: Sometimes loses your position at the setup stage, so the guitar is in the middle of your body. Needs more songs. Other than that it is hilarious, even if you don't want to play it, get someone else to and just watch. It has some unique twists as a departure from what you expect from guitar hero like games, VR is used to good effect, to have missed notes drift off on their own path that you still have to play, and freeform sections for lead are fun and silly and sound great. I just want crazy train.

developer_response.body:
Hi there NFHimself,

We updated the game again! Songs are coming by the end of this month! Yayyy!

Last Friday, we updated the game based on your concerns and thoughts. Thanks! These are the main things we have improved: 

	- Better the audio feedback on played and missed notes. The guitar will stop playing when you mess it. 
	- Better audio sync; Music latency is so much better now! The notes match with the music by default!!
	- Better strumming accuracy
	- Smoother guitar movement when playing to make the experience way more stable. 
	- Cloud saves. Never lose your progress. 

We also fixed several bugs reported by the community.
We hope to see you back on the stage soon to feel the improvements to Unplugged. 
Thanks for your patience. 
We are incredibly thankful to have you on the road with us!

Think this game needs a calibration setting and everyone’s different in there timing but practice makes perfect. Overall I’m really enjoying this game and probably keep playing for hours

reviews.review_description: Just downloaded today and it’s taking me back to my guitar hero days. Had a little tough time at first dealing how to lower the guitar but once I figured that out I’ve been having lots of fun. Keep it up!

Been having a blast with this game and it's the game I come back to the most lately! The hand tracking works surprisingly well, although it can be slightly finicky at times.

A tip to help the hand tracking work its best is to press your fingers into your palm corresponding to the chord shape being played. Also, when your fingers aren't being used for a chord make sure to pull them away from your palm as far as possible.

As someone that grew up as a Guitar Hero fanatic, this game scratches that same itch quite nicely. Highly recommended!
